holdings.infoBox: GENZ is an equity ETF with a total of 35 individual holdings. The top holdings are NetEase stock at 8.87%, Uber Technologies at 7.83%, The Charles Schwab Corporation at 7.53%, Shopify at 7.41%, and Electronic Arts at 7.40%.

overview.description: The VanEck Digital Native Economy ETF (GENZ) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the MarketVector Digital Native Economy index, a market-cap-weighted index of US-listed companies considered to be shaping the next-generation economy GENZ was launched on Jan 22, 2008 and is issued by VanEck.

data.title: VanEck Converts Gaming ETF into GENZ ETF Targeting Digital Native Economy
data.text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--VanEck relaunches Gaming ETF as VanEck Digital Native Economy ETF (GENZ), targeting structural changes across payments, work and online engagement.

data.title: VanEck Announces Year-End Distributions for VanEck Equity ETFs
data.text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--VanEck announced today the 2025 annual distributions per share for its VanEck equity exchange-traded funds.

data.title: VanEck turns online slang into strategy with 'Degen Economy' ETF
data.text: The firm is renaming its gaming ETF (BJK) the "VanEck Degen Economy ETF."

data.title: VanEck Announces Yearend Distributions for VanEck Equity ETFs
data.text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--VanEck announced today the following 2022 annual distributions per share for its VanEck® equity exchange-traded funds.

data.title: Online gambling is sending sports betting ETFs to record highs
data.text: ETF players are doubling down on the online gambling and sports betting arena.

data.title: Casino Stocks Appear Set to Head Higher
data.text: Bullish chart patterns suggest that gaming-related stock prices could be headed higher over the weeks and months ahead.

data.title: Best Gaming ETFs for Q4 2020
data.text: The best gaming ETFs for Q4 2020 are ESPO, NERD, and BJK.

data.title: Easing Macau travel restrictions propel casino stocks and gaming ETFs
data.text: Exchange-traded funds with exposure to casino stocks jumped Monday after Chinese authorities announced plans to loosen travel restrictions on people returning to the mainland from Macau.
